Name: mixed_precision_bandwidth_optimization
Title: Maximizing Memory Bandwidth Utilization through Adaptive Mixed-Precision Quantization on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: Adaptive mixed-precision quantization can be optimized to specifically enhance memory bandwidth utilization, leading to measurable improvements in throughput and latency on commodity GPUs.
Related Work: Existing methods such as MxMoE and MicroMix focus on mixed-precision quantization but often overlook the importance of tailored memory bandwidth optimization. This proposal distinguishes itself by directly correlating quantization strategies with memory bandwidth utilization metrics, which is less explored in the current literature.
Abstract: In the context of deploying large-scale machine learning models on commodity GPUs, memory bandwidth utilization remains a critical bottleneck affecting inference performance. This proposal introduces a novel approach to mixed-precision quantization that adapts to memory bandwidth constraints by dynamically adjusting quantization levels based on real-time profiling of memory access patterns. We will investigate the impact of adaptive mixed-precision quantization on throughput and latency, employing a profiling methodology that focuses on kernel-level metrics, such as achieved bandwidth and memory coalescing. Our experiments will benchmark several popular models across various datasets, measuring performance improvements in terms of tokens-per-second and energy efficiency. This research aims to provide a concrete framework for optimizing inference on commodity hardware, ultimately contributing to the deployment of more efficient deep learning solutions.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline mixed-precision quantization strategy on a selected model (e.g., ResNet or BERT) and measure its performance using standard profiling tools.
2. Develop an adaptive mixed-precision quantization algorithm that adjusts quantization levels based on memory bandwidth utilization metrics collected from profiling.
3. Conduct A/B latency races between the baseline and adaptive strategies under fixed accuracy budgets, measuring p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token.
4. Analyze the impact of different quantization schemes on achieved memory bandwidth and kernel throughput, using nvprof/Nsight for detailed profiling.
5. Compare results against existing state-of-the-art mixed-precision methods to quantify improvements.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The adaptive quantization algorithm may introduce overhead that could negate some performance gains if not optimized properly.
Variability in hardware conditions (e.g., GPU model, driver versions) can affect reproducibility and may require careful control.
The focus on memory bandwidth could overlook other potential bottlenecks, necessitating a balanced approach to optimization.
